questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: The salaries of private sector employees has nearly doubled in the last two years.
Courses of action: I. Higher income tax rate should be imposed on private sector employees.
II. The wages of public sector employees should be doubled.
III. A further rise should not be allowed.

choices.e: At least one of the above follows

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Some bottles of cold drinks have been found to contain insects, flies and even lizards.
Courses of action: I. People should be told about these incidents.
II. Cold drink manufacturers should be punished for not providing proper care.
III. The sale of cold drinks should be banned.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: There has been a record jump in Kharif crop this year as a result of timely monsoon.
Courses of action: I. An enquiry commission should be set up to study the technical reasons of the jump.
II. Efforts should be made to maintain the growth of the crops.
III. The country should take adequate steps for food storing facilities.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: The economic policies of the government are being criticized by some economists as being opposed to the concept of social equality.
Courses of action: I. Since capitalism and social equality can't go together, the government need not bother about such criticisms.
II. The government should evaluate such criticisms on any possible merit.
III. The economic policies should be changed in such a way that social equality is included in its objective.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: There has been a spate of kidnapping in Delhi over the recent months.
Courses of action: I. The law and order situation should be tightened.
II. An anti-kidnapping squad should be formed within the police.
III. People should not be allowed to travel alone and unprotected during night hours.

choices.d: Either I and II, or III follows

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: According to weather experts, heavy winds and storms are likely to occur in the coastal areas of Andhra Pradesh and Kerala.
Courses of action: I. The weather experts should be awarded for their timely warnings.
II. The fishermen of these areas should be forewarned not to venture into the sea.
III. The government should not air such reports because they disturb the people unnecessarily.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Despite the provision of legal punishments, the system of dowry remains prevalent in our society.
Courses of action: I. Focus should be diverted to proper execution of the legal provisions.
II. Stricter laws should be enforced by the Parliament against dowry.
III. The youth should be persuaded to rebel against their parents and indulge in no-dowry marriages.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: The elections have become expensive and complicated. One of the reasons is that anybody can stand for election; this sometimes results in an extremely large number of candidates and with it large public expenses.
Courses of action: I. Not more than five people should be allowed to contest from one constituency.
II. A commission should be set up to suggest how to get over this problem without hurting the democratic nature of our elections.
III. Since, in a democracy, any citizen should be given the right to contest, we should accept the situation as inevitable.

choices.d: I or II or III follows
